skill.name: lark-im
skill.description: Use Lark IM through lark-cli to search conversations, send approved messages, manage group chats, handle reactions, and transfer message resources.
skill.summary: Operate Lark IM messaging and chat workflows from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code with authenticated lark-cli commands.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found they are markdown command examples, API paths, placeholder IDs, and references rather than hidden executable code. The skill is still medium risk because it intentionally guides authenticated lark-cli network operations that can read private messages, send messages, change chats, and upload or download files. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External CLI Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill requires lark-cli and documents commands that call Lark IM APIs. This is expected for the skill, but it lets an assistant perform authenticated actions in the user tenant.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence directly shows the required external binary and documented command pattern. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands are normal lark-cli usage.

medium_findings.title: Message Reading and Search Can Expose Private Content
medium_findings.description: The skill supports listing, searching, and enriching Lark messages across conversations. Results may include private chat content, sender details, mentions, and resource keys visible to the authenticated identity.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed documentation explicitly describes message listing, search, batch fetch, and chat enrichment. Legitimate access controls apply, but the data can be sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Message Sending and Chat Changes Affect Other Users
medium_findings.description: The skill can send messages, create chats, update chat metadata, and manage reactions. These actions are visible to other people and can modify shared workspace state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly describe write operations. The send workflow includes an explicit approval requirement, so this is a managed medium risk instead of a high-risk finding.

medium_findings.title: Local File Upload and Message Resource Download
medium_findings.description: The skill documents uploading local media when sending messages and downloading message resources to local paths. This requires care with file selection and downloaded content handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines directly document local file uploads and controlled downloads. The download reference limits outputs to relative paths and disallows traversal, reducing severity.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Matches Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Most scanner hits for Ruby backticks, path traversal, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance are caused by markdown code fences, shell quoting examples, API paths, placeholder IDs, and relative documentation links. No executable Ruby code or hidden script was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens occur in markdown documentation and command examples. Manual review found no evidence that these strings execute automatically.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly instructs assistants to run lark-cli commands. This is necessary for the integration but should remain permission-gated and scoped to user requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and expected. It becomes risky only when commands are run without proper user intent, identity checks, or approval for write actions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Network API Effects
dangerous_patterns.description: Documented shortcuts map to Lark Open API endpoints for sending, searching, and downloading message resources. These operations can expose or modify workspace data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The references directly name network-backed API operations. The behavior is legitimate for a Lark IM skill but needs user awareness.

content.value_statement: Teams need a reliable way to inspect Lark conversations and complete messaging tasks without switching tools. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through authenticated lark-cli workflows for messages, chats, reactions, and resources.
actual_capabilities: Search Lark messages by keyword, sender, chat, attachment type, and time range.
List group chat or direct message history with pagination and thread context.
Send approved bot messages as text, Markdown, post content, or media attachments.
Create and update group chats when the authenticated identity has permission.
Download image and file resources from messages to safe relative paths.
Inspect and manage emoji reactions through the documented Lark IM APIs.
limitations: Requires lark-cli, valid Lark authentication, and the correct app scopes.
Cannot bypass Lark tenant, membership, visibility, or permission limits.
Bot identity may not resolve sender names outside the app visibility range.
Markdown sending is converted to Feishu post format and may change formatting.

use_cases.title: Find Conversation Context
use_cases.description: Search recent Lark messages by keyword, sender, or time range before responding to an issue.

use_cases.title: Send Approved Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare and send a confirmed bot message to a project group or direct recipient.

use_cases.title: Recover Shared Files
use_cases.description: Locate file or image keys in message history and download the requested resource locally.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the most recent messages in the Lark group with chat ID oc_xxx. Use a read-only command and summarize the key points.

prompt_templates.title: Search With Filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Lark messages for the phrase release blocker between 2026-03-01 and 2026-03-10. Limit the search to group chats and show the sources.

prompt_templates.title: Draft And Send Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a short bot message for group oc_xxx about the deployment status. Ask me to confirm the recipient and text before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Create Group Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a private Lark group for the migration project, include me by default, add reachable members after creation, and report any users that could not be added.

output_examples.input: Search messages about the release plan in the platform group.
output: Found recent matches in the platform group and one related thread.
The main decision was to keep the release window on Friday.
Two follow-up owners were mentioned for testing and documentation.

output_examples.input: Send this deployment note to the engineering group.
output_examples.output: I will confirm the recipient, message text, and bot identity before sending. After approval, I will report the created message ID and timestamp.

output_examples.input: Download the image from this message.
output_examples.output: I found the image resource key in the message and saved the file to the requested relative path. I also checked that traversal paths were not used.

best_practices: Confirm recipient, message content, and sending identity before any write action.
Use the narrowest search filters that satisfy the request to limit data exposure.
Prefer dry-run or read-only commands when checking parameters or permissions.
anti_patterns: Do not send messages based only on implied intent or incomplete recipient details.
Do not search broad message history when a specific chat or time range is known.
Do not upload or download files unless the user identifies the exact resource.

faq.question: Does this skill send messages automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill documentation requires explicit user approval for recipient, content, and bot identity before sending.

faq.question: What authentication is required?
faq.answer: It requires lark-cli authentication and the Lark scopes needed for each requested IM operation.

faq.question: Can it read private messages?
faq.answer: It can read only conversations visible to the authenticated user or bot identity and allowed by Lark permissions.

faq.question: Can it download files from chats?
faq.answer: Yes. It can download message image or file resources when the user provides valid message and resource identifiers.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill runs authenticated network commands that can read messages, write messages, change chats, and move files.

faq.question: Does it include hidden executable code?
faq.answer: Manual review found documentation and command examples, but no hidden scripts or confirmed malicious behavior.
